content.artifact_title: Hamut'ay Probe Run 01c17b98 — Dual-Task Completion Synthesis
content.conclusion: Both tasks alpha and beta completed cleanly under the Hamut'ay event-loop probe framework. Task alpha (cycle 1–3) established the continuation-request pattern: probe ready, one item retained, framework-bound continuation requested and completed. Task beta (cycle 4–6) replicated this pattern faithfully and introduced a housekeeping audit mechanism confirming zero open items and clean status. Across both tasks, the probe maintained readiness throughout, no durable fields were lost or corrupted, and the framework-owned continuity fields (cycle, open_items, probe_status, continuation_request) carried forward without degradation. The dual-task run demonstrates reliable wake-state preservation, symbolic context handoff between cycles, and disciplined state hygiene. No losses declared.
